item.dc:creator: FutileHumility

item.title: Let’s not go backwards
item.description: I understand that Putin is homophobic and that calling him gay would be offensive to him. But it’s taking us back to a time when “gay” was an insult, and it’s sending the message that being gay is something shameful or worth being hated for. There are PLENTY of reasons to shit on Putin without insulting an already-marginalized population. *steps off soapbox* Edit: Did you all just… miss the first sentence of what I wrote? Edit 2: I want to remind everyone that Putin won’t see your posts on here. But young gay kids might. Having grown up in an environment where people WERE insulted to be called gay, I took a long time to realize that it wasn’t a character flaw and that I wasn’t worth less because of it. Of all the decorations in my class, the one that gets the most comments is my Pride flag, and kids literally come to me for advice/ validation. This stuff about “LGBT folks are fine with it” isn’t accurate. Just because you are, it doesn’t mean everyone is. #standwithukraine #fuckputin #lgbt
item.dc:creator: ptothedubs

item.title: Hey. We're having chocolate chip pancakes. Come on over, rest a while.
item.description: Tiny octopus wishes he could make pancakes for anyone who wanted them. Today especially. Normally it would be waffles, but there was an ice storm last night, so it's a pancake sort of morning. Be well everyone. Choose kindness.
item.dc:creator: TinyOctopus

item.title: 80-year-old showed up to join the army, carrying with him a small case with 2 tshirts, a pair of extra pants, a toothbrush and a few sandwiches for lunch. He said he was doing it for his grandkids.

item.title: Day 273: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: Aisle Nine Baconator Two 5oz Beef Patties, 2 Slices Of Cheddar, 4 Strips Of Thick Cut Applewood Smoked Bacon, Dill Pickles, Ketchup And Mayo, And Finished On A Charcoal Grill For That Outdoor Smokehouse Bbq From Aisle Nine Gourmet Burgers & Poutine In Greater Sudbury, ON, Canada Thank You @yodadude For This Burger Submission! 12/10! Previous Day 272: https://imgur.com/gallery/lUMqihi MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD

item.title: Ukraine Update Feb 25: Ukraine Stands Defiant.
item.description:
Damaged but not defeated: Victorious National Guard troops celebrate their victory over Russian elite VDV paratroopers at the Hostomel (Antonov) Airport. The war is in its early stages. In the south are Russian forces advancing quickly. Elsewhere Ukraine is putting up stiff resistance and has soundly defeated the Russian airborne assault at Hostomel. Russian advances have been largely confined to close to central roads and significant Ukrainian infantry remain in the Russian rear. The Ukrainian Armed Forces can muster over 600,000 men, have high morale, and are trained. If supported with Western arms, intelligence, and money Ukraine has a real, if slim, chance of denying Russia the conquest it seeks. The fight will be hard and bloody. What it is not is hopeless. Every attempt is made to ensure all presented information is accurate, complete, and presented with proper context. This post is very long.
As a reminder I will not be posting any Ukrainian troop movements or positions. Russian movements will be posted. I urge you to do the same.
Message from a Ukrainian infantry company. Unlike in Afghanistan or Iraq the morale of the Ukrainian soldier remains high. They are fighting.
American President Joe Biden announced another round of sanctions, again coordinated with US allies. Biden has done a good job with this crisis. He has supplied arms and intelligence to Ukraine. He has rallied NATO to the cause and been unprecedentedly open with US intelligence, proving to the world the true nature of Russian aggression. He has done well but will undoubtedly get unfairly blamed for not stopping Putin from invading.
NATO Article 4 has been activated. The article states: “The Parties will consult together whenever, in the opinion of any of them, the territorial integrity, political independence or security of any of the Parties is threatened.”
Russian T-72 tank outside of Kerson flying the Soviet flag.
The Federated States of Micronesia severs diplomatic relations with the Russian Federation due to the unprovoked invasion of Ukraine.
Mounting Russian losses will put Putin under real political pressure at home and the regime is willing to hide them any way they can. In the 2014 War Russia told the greaving mothers of dead soldiers that they died in training accidents.
Ukraine has committed to Total War. The entirety of the resources of the state are being thrown into the fight.
Russian students at St. Petersburg State Medical University are being called for military service.
Russian airbase at Millerovo burns after being struck by a Ukrainian Tochka-U tactical ballistic missile.
Civilians take shelter from Russia bombardment in the Kyiv metro.
Lines form at the enlistment offices in Volyn as Ukrainians answer the call to arms.
A Ukrainian father says goodbye to his children as they evacuate. He stays behind to fight.
Emergency services are out in Kyiv fighting fires and rescuing who they can. In Syria Russian forces have been known to do "double tap" strikes; striking an area and then hitting it again shortly after to kill first responders. So far we have yet to see that tactic in Ukraine.
Unconfirmed. If true this speaks volumes to the high morale and will to resist amongst the population.
The Ukrainian Air Force is still in the air and fighting. This MiG-29 has been dubbed the "Ghost of Kyiv" for its reported exploits with 6 claimed kills.
Prayer in the Great Choral Synagogue while Russia bombards Kyiv.
Ukrainian Mi-14 helicopter lands at the military hospital in Odessa to deliver wounded. That such evacuations are possible in the face of the Russian Air Force is a testament to Ukrainian courage and ability.
Canadian Defense Chief: "For the close to 3,000 members of the Canadian Armed Forces who have served on Operation UNIFIER since 2015, this may be an especially difficult time. Your thoughts, like mine, will no doubt be with your friends and comrades as they face the ultimate test. This is only natural. ... The world has just become a much more dangerous place, and we must do all that we can to stand as one and be ready."
Russian forces cross from Belarus into Ukraine at the Three Sisters Crossing.
At the tiny Zmiinyi Island the Ukrainian Border Guards refuse to surrender. All 13 perished. All where posthumously granted the title of Hero of Ukraine.
"In the name of humanity bring your troops back to Russia." - UN
A destroyed Russian Tor-M1 air defense system.
Elena Kovalskaya, the director of Moscow’s Vsevolod Meyerhold State Theater and Cultural Center, has announced her resignation in protest against the invasion of Ukraine. “It’s impossible to work for a murderer and collect a salary from him,”
Kyiv.
Putin has been trying to use nuclear blackmail to scare the West into inaction. This is a bluff. The West must remember that it has many options that fall far bellow the nuclear threshold.
"I decided to conduct a special military operation. Its goal is the protection of people..." - Vladimir Putin.
Small acts such as this do add up and it is important to make isolating aggressor nations the accepted norm.
Russian forces cross a pontoon bridge over the Pripyat river in the Belarusian Chernobyl Exclusion Zone.
Increased radiation levels around Chernobyl. The levels are still minor but reports of Russian shelling hitting a waste repository are extremely concerning.
Women: Who are you? Soldier: We have exercises here. Please go this way. Women: What kind of exercises? Are you Russian? Soldier: Yes. Women: So what the fuck are you doing here? Soldier: Right now, our discussion will lead to nothing. Women: You're occupants, you're fascists! What the fuck are you doing on our land with all these guns? Take these seeds and put them in your pockets, so at least sunflowers [Ukrainian national flower] will grow when you all lie down here. Soldier:Right now, our discussion will lead nowhere. Let's not escalate this situation. Please. Women: What situation? Guys, guys. Put the sunflower seeds in your pockets, please. You will lie down here with the weeds. You came to my land. Do you understand? You are occupiers. You are enemies. Soldier: Yes. Women: And from this moment you are cursed. I'm telling you.
The People's Daily, the Chinese state newspaper, does not report on the invasion.
An American RQ-4 Global Hawk keeps a close eye on Russian amphibious forces in the black sea.
The great thing about free, prosperous, societies is that people actually want to live there. The West has a wide range of policy options available. Do not fall into the trap of "Russia has nukes therefore we should do nothing".
Ukrainian civilians liberate fuel from a Russian BTR abandoned after the a failed attack on Kharkov.
A KamAZ 5350 of the Russian 4th Guard Tank Division.
[Audio only] A British born volunteer with the Ukrainian Forces records the fighting at Hostomel (Antonov) Airport.
Russians take to the street to protest against the war. Latest reports are that over 1,000 have been arrested.
Riot police in St. Petersburg drag away peaceful anti-war protesters.
Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida: "It is an extremely serious situation with ramifications for the international order, not just in Europe but Asia and beyond... Japan needs to show its resolve not to allow any change to the status quo by force."
Ukrainian PRV-13 'Odd Pair burns after being struck.
Russian MT-LB destroyed outside of Kharkiv.
The first wartime address of President Zelenskyy on Feb 24: "If you, dear European leaders, dear world leaders, leaders of the free world, do not help us today, then tomorrow the war will knock on your door."
The second wartime address of President Zelenskyy on Feb 25: "Stop the enemy wherever you see it. The fate of Ukraine depends only on Ukrainians. No one but ourselves will control our lives. We are on our land, the truth is on our side. It will not be possible to destroy our character. Kalibr missiles are helpless against our freedom." President Zelenskyy has vowed to remain in Kyiv for the duration of the conflict.
American ground and air power is on its way to Europe. This will be needed to prevent any "spillover" of fighting and to bolster European security in the face of a drastically destabilized situation.
The Finnish Defence Forces have started investigating what weapons or other defense materiel it could hand over without undermining its own defense capabilities. Minister of Defence Kaikkonen said Finland is “positive” about requests from other countries to provide Ukraine weapons and other supplies acquired from Finland. Finnish President Niinistö says Finland is not considering joining NATO at this time. “We’ll draw conclusions after the crisis about what measures are needed.”
"Estonia is protected. Our own defense forces, together with other NATO units, keep our borders secure. But at least with comprehensive assistance, be it civilian, military or humanitarian, we must also help protect and preserve Ukraine. By keeping the hope of the Ukrainian people alive, we also preserve Estonia!"
Lithuania recognizes the simple reality of the situation: Ukraine is at war and war are fought with weapons.
"Putin and his oligarch friends seek a divided world and the destruction of democracy." Senator Bernie Sanders condemns the Russian invasion, calls for more sanctions, unity with allies and for the US to accept refugees.
Jarad Polis, the Governor of the American State of Colorado condemns the invasion. He announces Colorado is willing to accept refuges, will be canceling contracts with Russian business, and will divest all investments in Russian assets. A good reminder of the power American States have and an example to follow.
Ukrainian Ambassador to Russian ambassador at UN: “There is no purgatory for war criminals, they go straight to hell ambassador”
The long feared war is upon Europe. Ukraine is standing against Russia. Not only does the West have a moral duty to assist Ukraine but in even the cold logic of realpolitik this is the time to act; blood is expensive and weapons are cheap and it is Ukraine who is paying the toll of blood. Western weapons, intelligence, and money combined with Ukrainian will and soldiers may yet deny Russia the victory it seeks. Sanctions are good, and accepting refuges is even better. The harshest of sanctions will effect the West as well as Russia. That can not be an excuse to not implement them. The eyes of history are upon us. The chance of success may be slim but that is no excuse to shirk from duty. Act in whatever way you can, however small it may be. This war, like every war, is a tragedy. We must look at it with sober and open eyes to know the cost and to be firm in the conviction to take every action needed to prevent such wars from occurring. Recognize misinformation. Stay informed. Stay safe. Разом переможемо! Слава Україні та її Воїнам!
item.dc:creator: RedE100

item.title: A little help from a friend
item.dc:creator: biokleen
